Speaker 1: Now let's join Anna back at her treehouse.
Speaker 1: She shows us a useful trick.
Speaker 1: She adds a letter to weather words and changes them.
Speaker 1: See the changes the letter Y makes.
Speaker 2: The letter Y at the end of a word changes it.
Speaker 2: It changes weather nouns to weather adjectives.
Speaker 2: A rainy day has rain.
Speaker 2: We feel wind on a windy day.
Speaker 2: A cloudy sky has many clouds.
Speaker 2: A foggy day has lots of fog.
Speaker 2: The letter Y also changes a one-sound word into a two-sound word.
Speaker 2: Listen sun sun knee rain rainy storm stormy
Speaker 2: Storm is a noun.
Speaker 2: Stormy is an adjective.
Speaker 2: It describes weather.
Speaker 2: A storm can have snow, wind, thunder, lightning, and rain
Speaker 2: For rain, you need an umbrella.
Speaker 1: What did the leather Y do?
Speaker 1: It changed a noun into an adjective.
Speaker 1: Adjectives describe nouns.
Speaker 1: It also changed a one-sound word into a two-sound word.
